cards.front: How do you say 'I want to [verb]' in Korean?
cards.grammarNote: -고 싶어요 attaches directly to the verb stem with no vowel harmony: 먹다 → 먹고 싶어요, 마시다 → 마시고 싶어요, 하다 → 하고 싶어요. It does not change based on the stem vowel.

cards.front: I want to try Korean food

cards.grammarNote: To negate -고 싶어요, change 싶어요 to 싶지 않아요: 먹고 싶지 않아요 (don't want to eat). The -고 part stays the same.

cards.front: I don't want to drink coffee
I want a coffee (wanting a thing, not an action)
cards.grammarNote: For wanting a noun/thing, use 원해요 (from 원하다) or [noun]이/가 좋아요. Use -고 싶어요 only when 'wanting to do' an action.

cards.front: I want to eat something sweet
I want to go to that restaurant
She wanted to eat (third person past desire)
cards.grammarNote: -고 싶어했어요 is used for third-person desires (he/she/they wanted to). First person uses -고 싶어요; third person uses -고 싶어하다 conjugated normally.

cards.front: I want to cook at home today
